license.declaredUseBoundary: personal study, research, technical exchange, and non-commercial material organization
license.thirdPartyRightsNotice: Courses, screenshots, transcriptions, PDF text, and related materials remain subject to their respective rights holders and may require separate permission.

evidenceArchitecture.courseDistillation: Secondary summaries and study modules derived from Ni Haisha courses.
evidenceArchitecture.screenshotEvidence: 2,986 repository WebP images indexed by course and timestamp; useful to verify that a board or slide appeared, not that its claim is true.

pdfEvidence.finding: Stable page-card IDs and extracted page text are useful locators, but the manifest does not identify an edition, publisher, publication year, source URL, or source-file hash for any of the 22 PDFs.

classicalSignalAudit.method: Count unique page-evidence records whose extracted text contains the simplified or traditional title form. These are locator signals, not verified quotations or complete-work coverage.

findings.reason: Matches occur in Tianji raw witness or textbook files. A correction made to upstream ASR or production prose cannot authorize rewriting a separate witness.
The upstream decision concerns a specific formula or course context; Tianji occurrences are not the same claim.
The upstream repository normalized its own production layer to 兼证, but this is not a primary textual witness or sufficient authority to rewrite Tianji by itself.

classicalValidationRouting.externalUse: Use the 207 page-card signals and course screenshots to locate cited passages, transcription differences, and teaching claims.
classicalValidationRouting.authorityBoundary: No standalone Lingshu source is registered upstream; course or later-book references cannot replace an independently identified Lingshu edition.

classicalValidationRouting.externalUse: Use the 227 page-card signals to find course claims and candidate chapter numbers for independent checking.
classicalValidationRouting.authorityBoundary: The upstream PDF manifest has no standalone Nanjing document; title mentions inside course and later books are secondary locators only.

classicalValidationRouting.externalUse: Use course page cards, Ni commentary, recommended-book indexes, and correction decisions to locate clause variants and suspected transcription errors.
classicalValidationRouting.authorityBoundary: Course transcripts, Ni commentary, mixed commentary/translation PDFs, and extracted DOC clauses must remain separate from the classical base text.

tianjiTargets: database/tcm/skill-v3/references/45-经典-金匮要略杂病方证条文.md
classicalValidationRouting.externalUse: Use the two course PDFs and the separately labeled recommended supplement to locate chapter, formula, and wording candidates.
classicalValidationRouting.authorityBoundary: The recommended supplement explicitly mixes original text, notes, translation, and later explanation; it cannot be treated as a uniform classical witness.

promotionRequirements: identify an independent edition-level classical witness with stable bibliographic metadata
bind each candidate claim to work, chapter or clause, edition, locator, and quoted text
separate course speech, transcription, commentary, later translation, OCR, and classical text
verify source-file identity and rights before copying any upstream content
keep medical actions, doses, diagnosis, prognosis, and cases blocked from product use
